query: From these transcripts, identify two explicit years: (1) the year the narrator uses to describe the bedrooms or rooms at the ranch, and (2) the year Tombstone founder Ed Schieffelin died, if that death year is explicitly stated in the transcripts. Then compute their sum. If either year is not explicitly present, say which one is missing instead of guessing.

metadata.focus: monument to Tombstone founder Ed Schieffelin and related history

transcripts.text: When you're walking around, you'll feel it. And it's just like walking the streets of Tombstone. That famous town is just two miles away. We'll take you for a visit in just a bit. But even closer stands the namesake of Tombstone Monument Ranch, the 25-foot tall stone monument to Tombstone founder Ed Sheflin. The Apaches ruled this country when the prospector came here looking for gold. Friends warned all he'd find in the desert would be his own tombstone. And he came close one day when he hid out from an Apache war party in some rocks where that monument now stands, which you can visit on a trail ride. But when Sheflin discovered the silver strike that made him rich, he had the last laugh, naming his new town
